volumeTitle: Persians. Seven against Thebes. Suppliants. Prometheus Bound
label: Prometheus Bound (Section 690 - Section 835)

sections.label: Section 690
sections.original: οὐδ ὧδε δυσθέατα καὶ [*] δύσοιστα
sections.translation: or that sufferings so grievous to look upon , yes , and so grievous to endure , a tale of outrage , would strike my soul as if with double-pronged goad . Alas , O Fate , O Fate ,

sections.label: Section 695
sections.original: πέφρικ εἰσιδοῦσα [*] πρᾶξιν Ἰοῦς .
sections.translation: I shudder to behold the plight that has befallen Io .

sections.label: Section 696
sections.original: πρῴ γε στενάζεις καὶ φόβου πλέα τις εἶ :
sections.translation: You lament and are full of fear all too soon . Wait until you have learned the rest as well .

sections.label: Section 698
sections.original: λέγ , ἐκδίδασκε : τοῖς νοσοῦσί τοι γλυκὺ
sections.translation: Proceed , tell all . It is comforting for the sick to know clearly beforehand what pain still awaits them .

sections.label: Section 700
sections.original: τὴν πρίν γε χρείαν [*] ἠνύσασθ ἐμοῦ πάρα
sections.translation: You gained your former request easily from me ; for you first desired the story of her ordeal from her own lips . Hear now the sequel , the sufferings this maid is fated to endure at Hera ’ s hand .

sections.label: Section 705
sections.original: σύ τ , Ἰνάχειον σπέρμα , τοὺς ἐμοὺς λόγους
sections.translation: And may you , daughter of Inachus , lay to heart my words so that you may learn the end of your wanderings . First , from this spot , turn yourself toward the rising sun and make your way over untilled plains ; and you shall reach the Scythian nomads , who dwell

sections.label: Section 710
sections.original: πεδάρσιοι ναίουσ [*] ἐπ εὐκύκλοις ὄχοις ,
sections.translation: in thatched houses , perched aloft on strong-wheeled wagons and are equipped with far-darting bows . Do not approach them , but keeping your feet near the rugged shore , where the sea breaks with a roar , pass on beyond their land . On the left hand dwell the workers in iron ,

sections.label: Section 715
sections.original: οἰκοῦσι Χάλυβες , οὓς φυλάξασθαί σε χρή .
sections.translation: the Chalybes , and you must beware of them , since they are savage and are not to be approached by strangers . Then you shall reach the river Hybristes , [*] which does not belie its name . Do not cross this , for it is hard to cross , until you come to Caucasus itself ,

sections.label: Section 720
sections.original: ὕψιστον , ἔνθα ποταμὸς ἐκφυσᾷ μένος
sections.translation: loftiest of mountains , where from its very brows the river pours out its might in fury . You must pass over its crests , which neighbor the stars , and enter upon a southward course , where you shall reach the host of the Amazons , who loathe all men . They shall in time to come

sections.label: Section 725
sections.original: κατοικιοῦσιν [*] ἀμφὶ Θερμώδονθ , ἵνα
sections.translation: inhabit Themiscyra on the Thermodon , where , fronting the sea , is Salmydessus ’ rugged jaw , evil host of mariners , step-mother of ships . The Amazons will gladly guide you on your way . Next , just at the narrow portals of the harbor , you shall reach

sections.label: Section 730
sections.original: Κιμμερικὸν ἥξεις , ὃν θρασυσπλάγχνως σε χρὴ
sections.translation: the Cimmerian isthmus . This you must leave with stout heart and pass through the channel of Maeotis ; and ever after among mankind there shall be great mention of your passing , and it shall be called after you the Bosporus . [*] Then , leaving the soil of Europe ,

sections.label: Section 735
sections.original: ἤπειρον ἥξεις Ἀσιάδ . ἆρ ὑμῖν δοκεῖ
sections.translation:
you shall come to the Asian continent .
Does it not seem to you that the tyrant of the gods is violent in all his ways ? For this god , desirous of union with this mortal maid , has imposed upon her these wanderings . Maiden , you have gained a cruel suitor

sections.label: Section 740
sections.original: μνηστῆρος . οὓς γὰρ νῦν ἀκήκοας λόγους ,
sections.translation: for your hand . As to the tale you now have heard — understand that it has not even passed the introduction .

sections.label: Section 742
sections.original: ἰώ μοί μοι , ἐή .
sections.translation: Ah me , ah me , alas !

sections.label: Section 743
sections.original: σὺ δ αὖ κέκραγας κἀναμυχθίζει : τί που
sections.translation: What ! You are crying and groaning again ? What will you do , I wonder , when you have learned the sufferings still in store for you ?

sections.label: Section 745
sections.original: ἦ γάρ τι λοιπὸν τῇδε πημάτων ἐρεῖς ;
sections.translation: What ! Can it be that you have sufferings still left to recount to her ?

sections.label: Section 746
sections.original: δυσχείμερόν γε πέλαγος ἀτηρᾶς δύης .
sections.translation: Yes , a tempestuous sea of calamitous distress .

sections.label: Section 747
sections.original: τί δῆτ ἐμοὶ ζῆν κέρδος , ἀλλ οὐκ ἐν τάχει
sections.translation: What gain have I then in life ? Why did I not hurl myself straightaway from this rugged rock , so that I was dashed to earth and freed from

sections.label: Section 750
sections.original: ἀπηλλάγην ; κρεῖσσον γὰρ εἰσάπαξ θανεῖν
sections.translation: all my sufferings ? It is better to die once and for all than linger out all my days in misery .

sections.label: Section 752
sections.original: ἦ δυσπετῶς [*] ἂν τοὺς ἐμοὺς ἄθλους φέροις ,
sections.translation: Ah , you would hardly bear my agonies to whom it is not foredoomed to die ; for death would have freed me from my sufferings .

sections.label: Section 755
sections.original: νῦν δ οὐδέν ἐστι τέρμα μοι προκείμενον
sections.translation: But now no limit to my tribulations has been appointed until Zeus is hurled from his sovereignty .

sections.label: Section 757
sections.original: ἦ γάρ ποτ ἔστιν ἐκπεσεῖν ἀρχῆς Δία ; [*]
sections.translation: What ! Shall Zeus one day be hurled from his dominion ?

sections.label: Section 758
sections.original: ἥδοι ἄν , [*] οἶμαι , τήνδ ἰδοῦσα συμφοράν .
sections.translation: You would rejoice , I think , to see that happen .

sections.label: Section 759
sections.original: πῶς δ οὐκ ἄν , ἥτις ἐκ Διὸς πάσχω κακῶς ;
sections.translation: Why not , since it is at the hand of Zeus that I suffer ?

sections.label: Section 760
sections.original: ὡς τοίνυν ὄντων τῶνδέ σοι μαθεῖν [*] πάρα .
sections.translation: Then you may assure yourself that these things are true .

sections.label: Section 761
sections.original: πρὸς τοῦ τύραννα σκῆπτρα συληθήσεται ;
sections.translation: By whom shall he be despoiled of the sceptre of his sovereignty ?

sections.label: Section 762
sections.original: πρὸς αὐτὸς αὑτοῦ κενοφρόνων βουλευμάτων .
sections.translation: By himself and his own empty-headed purposes .

sections.label: Section 763
sections.original: ποίῳ τρόπῳ ; σήμηνον , εἰ μή τις βλάβη .
sections.translation: In what way ? Oh tell me , if there be no harm in telling .

sections.label: Section 764
sections.original: γαμεῖ γάμον τοιοῦτον ᾧ ποτ ἀσχαλεῖ . [*]
sections.translation: He shall make a marriage that shall one day cause him distress .

sections.label: Section 765
sections.original: θέορτον , ἢ βρότειον ; εἰ ῥητόν , φράσον .
sections.translation: With a divinity or with a mortal ? If it may be told , speak out .

sections.label: Section 766
sections.original: τί δ ὅντιν ; οὐ γὰρ ῥητὸν αὐδᾶσθαι τόδε .
sections.translation: Why ask with whom ? I may not speak of this .

sections.label: Section 767
sections.original: ἦ πρὸς δάμαρτος ἐξανίσταται θρόνων ;
sections.translation: Is it by his consort that he shall be dethroned ?

sections.label: Section 768
sections.original: ἣ τέξεταί γε παῖδα φέρτερον πατρός .
sections.translation: Yes , since she shall bear a son mightier than his father .

sections.label: Section 769
sections.original: οὐδ ἔστιν αὐτῷ τῆσδ ἀποστροφὴ τύχης ;
sections.translation: And has he no means to avert this doom ?

sections.label: Section 770
sections.original: οὐ δῆτα , πλὴν ἔγωγ ἂν ἐκ δεσμῶν λυθείς .
sections.translation: No , none — except me , if I were released from bondage .

sections.label: Section 771
sections.original: τίς οὖν ὁ λύσων σ [*] ἐστὶν ἄκοντος Διός ;
sections.translation: Who then is to release you against the will of Zeus ?

sections.label: Section 772
sections.original: τῶν σῶν τιν αὐτὸν ἐκγόνων [*] εἶναι χρεών .
sections.translation: It is to be one of your own grandchildren .

sections.label: Section 773
sections.original: πῶς εἶπας ; ἦ μὸς παῖς σ ἀπαλλάξει κακῶν ;
sections.translation: What did you say ? A child of mine will release you from your misery ?

sections.label: Section 774
sections.original: τρίτος γε γένναν πρὸς δέκ ἄλλαισιν γοναῖς .
sections.translation: The third in descent after ten generations .

sections.label: Section 775
sections.original: ἥδ οὐκέτ εὐξύμβλητος ἡ χρησμῳδία .
sections.translation: Your prophecy is not easy to understand .

sections.label: Section 776
sections.original: καὶ μηδὲ σαυτῆς γ [*] ἐκμαθεῖν ζήτει πόνους .
sections.translation: Yes , so do not seek to learn the full extent of your own sufferings .

sections.label: Section 777
sections.original: μή μοι προτείνων κέρδος εἶτ ἀποστέρει .
sections.translation: Do not offer me a favor and then withdraw it .

sections.label: Section 778
sections.original: δυοῖν λόγοιν σε θατέρῳ δωρήσομαι .
sections.translation: I will present you with one or other of two tales .

sections.label: Section 779
sections.original: ποίοιν ; πρόδειξον , αἵρεσίν τ ἐμοὶ δίδου .
sections.translation: Which two ? Set them forth and offer me the choice .

sections.label: Section 780
sections.original: δίδωμ : ἑλοῦ γάρ , ἢ πόνων τὰ λοιπά σοι
sections.translation: I am making the offer : choose whether I shall reveal the sufferings still in store for you or the one who will be my deliverer .

sections.label: Section 782
sections.original: τούτων σὺ τὴν μὲν τῇδε , τὴν δ ἐμοὶ χάριν
sections.translation: Consent to bestow on her one of these favors , and on me the other ; do not deny me the tale . Tell her about her further wanderings ;

sections.label: Section 785
sections.original: ἐμοὶ δὲ τὸν λύσοντα : τοῦτο γὰρ ποθῶ .
sections.translation: tell me who will deliver you — for I would like to know this .

sections.label: Section 786
sections.original: ἐπεὶ προθυμεῖσθ , οὐκ ἐναντιώσομαι
sections.translation: Well , since you are bent on this , I will not refuse to proclaim all that you still crave to know . First , to you , Io , will I declare your much-vexed wandering , and may you engrave it on the recording tablets of your mind .

sections.label: Section 790
sections.original: ὅταν περάσῃς ῥεῖθρον ἠπείροιν [*] ὅρον ,
sections.translation: When you have crossed the stream that bounds the two continents , toward the flaming east , where the sun walks ,...... crossing the surging sea until you reach the Gorgonean plains of Cisthene , where the daughters of Phorcys dwell , ancient maids ,

sections.label: Section 795
sections.original: αἱ Φορκίδες ναίουσι δηναιαὶ κόραι
sections.translation: three in number , shaped like swans , possessing one eye amongst them and a single tooth ; neither does the sun with his beams look down upon them , nor ever the nightly moon . And near them are their three winged sisters , the snake-haired Gorgons , loathed of mankind ,

sections.label: Section 800
sections.original: ἃς θνητὸς οὐδεὶς εἰσιδὼν ἕξει πνοάς :
sections.translation: whom no one of mortal kind shall look upon and still draw breath . Such is the peril that I bid you to guard against . But now listen to another and a fearsome spectacle . Beware of the sharp-beaked hounds of Zeus that do not bark , the gryphons ,

sections.label: Section 805
sections.original: Ἀριμασπὸν ἱπποβάμον , οἳ χρυσόρρυτον
sections.translation: and the one-eyed Arimaspian folk , mounted on horses , who dwell about the flood of Pluto ’ s [*] stream that flows with gold . Do not approach them . Then you shall come to a far-off country of a dark race that dwells by the waters of the sun , where the river Aethiop is .

sections.label: Section 810
sections.original: τούτου παρ ὄχθας ἕρφ , ἕως ἂν ἐξίκῃ
sections.translation: Follow along its banks until you reach the cataract , where , from the Bybline mountains , Nile sends forth his hallowed and sweet stream . He will conduct you on your way to the three-angled land of Nilotis , where , at last , it is ordained for you ,

sections.label: Section 815
sections.original: Ἰοῖ , πέπρωται σοί τε καὶ τέκνοις κτίσαι .
sections.translation:
O Io , and for your children to found your far-off colony .
If anything of this is confusing to you and hard to understand , may you question me yet again , and gain a clear account ; for I have more leisure than I crave .

sections.label: Section 819
sections.original: εἰ μέν τι τῇδε λοιπὸν ἢ παρειμένον
sections.translation: If there is anything still remaining or passed over

sections.label: Section 820
sections.original: ἔχεις γεγωνεῖν τῆς πολυφθόρου πλάνης ,
sections.translation: of her direful wandering that you have to tell , oh speak . But if you have told all , grant us in turn the favor we request — you probably have it still in memory .

sections.label: Section 823
sections.original: τὸ πᾶν πορείας ἥδε τέρμ ἀκήκοεν .
sections.translation: She has now heard the full end of her travels ; yet so she may know that she has heard no vain tale from me ,

sections.label: Section 825
sections.original: ἃ πρὶν μολεῖν δεῦρ ἐκμεμόχθηκεν φράσω ,
sections.translation:
I will describe the toils she has endured before she came here , giving this as a sure proof of my account .
Most of the weary tale I shall leave out and come to the very close of your wanderings .
For when you reached the Molossian plains

sections.label: Section 830
sections.original: τὴν αἰπύνωτόν τ ἀμφὶ Δωδώνην , ἵνα
sections.translation: and the sheer ridge that encircles Dodona , where lies the prophetic seat of Thesprotian Zeus and that marvel , passing all belief , the talking oaks , by which you clearly , and in no riddling terms , were saluted as the renowned

sections.label: Section 835
sections.original: μέλλουσ ἔσεσθαι : τῶνδε προσσαίνει σέ τι ; [*]
sections.translation: bride-to-be of Zeus ( is any of this pleasing to you ?), then , stung by the gadfly , you rushed along the pathway by the shore to the great gulf of Rhea , from where you are tossed in backward-wandering course ; and for all time to come a recess of the sea ,
